memoryArtifacts.headline: Paisley Abbey built on Walter FitzAlan's land, 1st Steward of Scotland
memoryArtifacts.description: Paisley Abbey, Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scotland built in 1163 on Walter FitzAlan's land, 1st steward of Scotland  User:Colin / Wikimedia Commons / CC BY-SA 3.0

memoryArtifacts.headline: Walter's name and title as it appears in a royal charter to Holyrood Abbey: "Walter filio alani Dapifero".

memoryArtifacts.description: Twelfth-century secular lordships on the western seaboard of Scotland. Walter's domain included the depicted regions of Strathgryfe, Renfrew, Mearns, and North Kyle. Clydesdale and South Kyle were royal lordships, whilst Cunningham was a Morville lordship.

memoryArtifacts.headline: Walter FitzAlan's Seal, 1st Steward of Scotland.
memoryArtifacts.description: Walter FitzAlan's Seal, 1st steward of Scotland. The image has been scanned from Descriptive catalogue of impressions from ancient Scottish seals, royal, baronial ecclesiastical, and municipal, embracing a period from a.d. 1094 to the commonwealth, plate 3, figure 1, by Henry Laing, published in 1850. This book was published in Edinburgh, Scotland. The illustration is not credited. The seal reads "SIGILLUM WALTERI FILLI ALANI DAPIFERI REG.".

popularStory.headline: Marriage
popularStory.description: Walter was married to Eschina de Londres, an apparent member of the Londres/London family. There is reason to suspect that she was also matrilineally descended from a family native to southern Scotla …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Walter Fitz Alan 1st High Steward of Scotland於1106年出生在英國史洛普爾奧斯維斯崔時，他的父親Alan Dapifer FitzFlaad28歲，母親Avelina de Hesdin31歲。他於1127年之前與Eschyna de Londiis de Molle在英國結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他於1135年移民到蘇格蘭波藍德。他於1138年登記服兵役。他去世於1177年2月2日在英國蘇格蘭斯可迪夏 波德斯梅露絲享年71歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭雷福福爾佩斯利佩斯利 艾比。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Walter Fitz Alan 1st High Steward of Scotland于1106年出生在英国史洛普爾奧斯維斯崔时，他的父亲Alan Dapifer FitzFlaad28岁，母亲Avelina de Hesdin31岁。他于1127年之前与Eschyna de Londiis de Molle在英国结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他于1135年移民到苏格兰波藍德。他于1138年登记服兵役。他去世于1177年2月2日在英国苏格兰斯可迪夏 波德斯梅露絲享年71岁，埋葬在英国苏格兰雷福福爾佩斯利佩斯利 艾比。

nameMeanings.name: Fitz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
Altered form of English   Fitch  .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

variants.name: Fitzgerald
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, Liam, Aidan, Bridgid, Caitlin, Donal, Fitz, Fitzgerald, Ciaran, Colum, Dermot.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Gerald (see Gerald ), ‘son of Gerald’. This is a very common Anglo-Norman name in Ireland, where it has been Gaelicized as Mac Gearailt. Irish bearers of the name claim descent from Gerald Fitzwalter de Windsor, constable of Pembroke Castle in the early 12th century, whose grandson Raymond (or Redmond) ‘le Gros’ FitzGerald (died after 1185) led the invasion of Ireland on behalf of Strongbow in 1170. A branch of the family held the title Knight of Glin from at least 1299–2011.

variants.name: Fitzwater
meanings: Irish: altered form of Fitzwalter, a patronymic from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Walter , meaning ‘son of Walter’.

History: This is the name of a family of Norman origin, descended from Godfrey, Count of Brionne (died c. 1010). Godfrey's grandson, Richard FitzGilbert, accompanied William the Conqueror and was made Lord of Clare. His great-grandson was Robert FitzWalter, one of the barons who guaranteed the Magna Carta in 1215.

variants.name: Fitzherbert
meanings: English and Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Herbert (see Herbert ), ‘son of Herbert’.

variants.name: Fitzhenry
meanings: Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Henry (see Henry ), ‘son of Henry’. This surname is common in Ireland (mainly Connacht), where it has been Gaelicized as Mac Éinrí. In at least some cases, it seems to have been adopted as a surname indicating the illegitimate offspring of a man called Henry.

variants.name: Fitzsimmons
meanings: Irish and English: variant of Fitzsimon, with post-medieval excrescent -s, from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + either of the personal names Simon or Sigmund (see Simon ), ‘son of Simon or Sigmund’. The name is also found in the Gaelicized form Mac Síomóin.

variants.name: Fitzgibbon
meanings: Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Gibbon, a Norman French pet form of Gilbert ; ‘son of Gibbon’. This name has long been a widespread and important surname in Ireland, and Gaelicized as Mac Giobúin. Sir Gilbert (Gibbon) FitzJohn, illegitimate son of John FitzGerald, 1st Baron Desmond (died 1261), was the ancestor of a line of Anglo-Norman aristocracy in Ireland, who acquired the surname Fitzgibbon with reference to him. Gilbert and his Fitzgibbon descendants were known as ‘the White Knights’ on account of their fair complexion.

variants.name: Fitzharris
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aileen.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Harry ‘son of Harry’. MacLysaght describes this as the name of a distinguished County Wexford family, also known as Fitzhenry .

variants.name: Fitzmaurice
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aidan, Ciaran, Colm.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Maurice (see Morris 1), ‘son of Maurice’. This surname is common in Ireland, where it has also been Gaelicized as Mac Muiris. The Irish family of Fitzmaurice share a common Norman ancestry with the Fitzgerald family. They can be traced to Thomas Fitzmaurice, whose brother Gerald was the ancestor of the Irish Fitzgerald dynasty. Their father was Maurice Fitzgerald, a Norman baron (died 1176).

variants.name: Fitzhugh
meanings: English (Northamptonshire): from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Hugh (see Hugh ), ‘son of Hugh’.

History: William Fitzhugh (1651–1701), from Bedford, England, emigrated to VA c. 1670 and established himself on the Potomac River in what was then Stafford County, VA, as a planter and exporter. He also practiced law, was a member of the Virginia House of Burgesses, and served in 1687 as lieutenant colonel of the county militia.

variants.name: Fitzroy
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
Altered form of English   Fitch  .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

nameMeanings.name: Alan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Allen  . This is the usual form of the personal name in England and Scotland.

variants.name: Alao
meanings: West African (Nigeria): unexplained.
Filipino: metonymic occupational name from a Hispanicized form of Ilokano alaw, a term denoting a method used to get back a lost spirit.
In some cases possibly also Portuguese (Alão): from the personal name Alão, an equivalent of Alan .

variants.name: Allen
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English, Old French personal name Alain, Alein (Old Breton Alan), from a Celtic personal name of great antiquity and obscurity. In England the personal name is now usually spelled Alan, the surname Allen; in Scotland the surname is more often Allan. From 1139 it was common in Scotland, where the surname also derives from Gaelic Ailéne, Ailín, from ail ‘rock’. The present-day frequency of the surname Allen in England and Ireland is partly accounted for by the popularity of the personal name among Breton followers of William the Conqueror, by whom it was imported first to Britain and then to Ireland. Saint Alan(us) was a 5th-century bishop of Quimper, who was a cult figure in medieval Brittany. Another Saint Al(l)an was a Cornish or Breton saint of the 6th century, to whom a church in Cornwall is dedicated.
English: occasionally perhaps from the rare Middle English femaje personal name Aline (Old French Adaline, Aaline), a pet form of ancient Germanic names in Adal-, especially Adalheidis (see Allis ).
French: variant of   Allain  , a cognate of 1 above, and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

nameMeanings.name: Walter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from wald ‘rule’ + heri, hari ‘army’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, superseding the native Old English form, Wealdhere. It was a very popular name in medieval England, normally pronounced ‘Water’.

variants.name: Walther
meanings: from wald ‘rule’ + heri ‘army’; name of the famous medieval minnesinger Walther von der Vogelweide ( c.1170– c.1230 ).

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When Walter Fitz Alan 1st High Steward of Scotland was born in 1106, in Oswestry, Shropshire, England, his father, Alan Dapifer FitzFlaad, was 28 and his mother, Avelina de Hesdin, was 31. He married Eschyna de Londiis de Molle before 1127, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. He immigrated to Cumberland, Scotland in 1135. He registered for military service in 1138. He died on 2 February 1177, in Melrose, Scottish Borders, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 71, and was buried in Paisley Abbey, Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.Paisley Abbey, Paisley, Renfrewshire, Sc: Paisley Abbey, Paisley, Renfrewshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Walter Fitz Alan 1st High Steward of Scotland est né en 1106, à Oswestry, Shropshire, Angleterre, son père, Alan Dapifer FitzFlaad, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Avelina de Hesdin, avait 31 ans. Il a épousé Eschyna de Londiis de Molle avant 1127, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Il a immigré au Cumberland, Écosse en 1135. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1138. Il est décédé le 2 février 1177, à Melrose, Scottish Borders, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 71 ans, et a été enterré à Paisley Abbey, Paisley, Renfrewshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Walter Fitz Alan 1st High Steward of Scotland 1106 in Oswestry, Shropshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Alan Dapifer FitzFlaad 28 und seine Mutter Avelina de Hesdin 31. Er hat vor 1127 in England Eschyna de Londiis de Molle geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Er ist 1135 nach Cumberland, Scotland eingewandert. Er hat sich 1138 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. Er starb am 2. Februar 1177 im Alter von 71 Jahren in Melrose, Scottish Borders, Schottland, United Kingdom und wurde in Paisley Abbey, Paisley, Renfrewshire, Schottland, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Walter Fitz Alan 1st High Steward of Scotland è nato il nel 1106 nel Oswestry, Shropshire, England, il suo padre, Alan Dapifer FitzFlaad, aveva 28 anni e il suo madre, Avelina de Hesdin, aveva 31 anni. Lui sposato Eschyna de Londiis de Molle prima del 1127, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Immigrato nel Cumberland, Scotland nel 1135. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1138. Morì il 2 febbraio 1177, nel Melrose, Scottish Borders, Scozia, Regno Unito, all'età di 71 anni, e fu sepolto a Paisley Abbey, Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scozia, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Walter Fitz Alan 1st High Steward of Scotlandが1106年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアオスウエストリーで生まれた時，父Alan Dapifer FitzFlaadは28歳，母Avelina de Hesdinは31歳であった。1127年以前にイギリスでEschyna de Londiis de Molleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1135年にスコットランドクウンバーランドに移住した。彼は1138年に兵役登録をした。彼は1177年2月2日にイギリススコットランドスコッティッシュ ボルダースメルローゼで71歳で死去し，イギリススコットランドレンフリューシュアイアペイスリーペイスリー アビーにて埋葬された。

ko.Paisley Abbey, Paisley, Renfrewshire, Sc: 영국 스코틀랜드 렌프레w실르 파이슬리 파이슬리 애브브이
ko.lifeSummary: Walter Fitz Alan 1st High Steward of Scotland(이)가 1106년 영국 슈럽실르 오스베스트리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Alan Dapifer FitzFlaad(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Avelina de Hesdin(은)는 31세였다. 그는 1127년 이전 영국에서 Eschyna de Londiis de Molle(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1135년에 스컷랜드 쿠벌랜드로 이민을 떠났다. 그는 1138년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 그는 1177년 2월 2일 영국 스코틀랜드 스콧이시 볼더스 멜로즈에서 71세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 렌프레w실르 파이슬리 파이슬리 애브브이에 묻혔다.

pt.Melrose, Scottish Borders, Scotland: Melrose, Scottish Borders, Escócia, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Walter Fitz Alan 1st High Steward of Scotland nasceu em 1106, em Oswestry, Shropshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Alan Dapifer FitzFlaad, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Avelina de Hesdin, tinha 31 anos. Ele casou-se com Eschyna de Londiis de Molle antes 1127, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ele imigrou para o Cumberland, Scotland em 1135. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1138. Ele faleceu em 2 de fevereiro de 1177, em Melrose, Scottish Borders, Escócia, United Kingdom, com 71 anos, e foi sepultado em Paisley Abbey, Paisley, Renfrewshire, Escócia, United Kingdom.

ru.Paisley Abbey, Paisley, Renfrewshire, Sc: пеисли эбби, пеисли, ренфрюшир, Шотландия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Walter Fitz Alan 1st High Steward of Scotland родился в 1106 г. в местности освестри, шропшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Alan Dapifer FitzFlaad было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Avelina de Hesdin, 31. Он женился на женщине по имени Eschyna de Londiis de Molle перед 1127 г. в местности Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Он иммигрировал в кумбирланд, Шотландия в 1135 году. В 1138 году он поступил на военную службу. Он умер 2 февраля 1177 г. в местности мелрос, скоттиш бордирс, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 71 год и был похоронен в местности пеисли эбби, пеисли, ренфрюшир, Шотландия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Walter Fitz Alan 1st High Steward of Scotland nació en 1106, en Oswestry, Shropshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Alan Dapifer FitzFlaad, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Avelina de Hesdin, tenía 31 años. Se casó con Eschyna de Londiis de Molle antes de 1127, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Inmigró a Cumberland, Scotland en 1135. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1138. Murió el 2 de febrero de 1177, en Melrose, Scottish Borders, Escocia, Reino Unido, a la edad de 71 años, y fue sepultado en Paisley Abbey, Paisley, Renfrewshire, Escocia, Reino Unido.
